source_pr.title: SQL digester: Modify sql digester logic for IN statement with single literal `IN (?) => IN (...)`
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?
Issue Number: ref #44298 

Problem Summary:
* Currently, binding rule with in (1,2,3) is not applied to statements with only one literal (1)

### What is changed and how it works?
The reason why the problem occurs is that the sql digester creates different normalized sql for (1,2,3) and (1).
in (1,2,3) => in (...)
in (1) => in (?)
Thus when the optimizer tries to find a matching binding rule, it fails.

I add an additional rule in sql digester to translate statements like `in (1)` to `in (...)` instead. 

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [X] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

1. Unit test
* Run `bindinfo_test`
```
cd bindinfo
go test -run TestBindingInListWithSingleLiteral --tags=intest
```

* Run `parser_test`
```
make parser_unit_test
```

2. Manual test steps (#44298 ):
```
mysql> create table t (a int);
Query OK, 0 rows affected (0.09 sec)

mysql> create binding for select * from t where a in(1, 2, 3) using select * from t where a in (1, 2, 3);
Query OK, 0 rows affected (0.01 sec)

mysql> select * from t where a in (1, 2,3);
Empty set (0.01 sec)

mysql> select @@last_plan_from_binding;
+--------------------------+
| @@last_plan_from_binding |
+--------------------------+
|                        1 |
+--------------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)

mysql> select * from t where a in (1, 2);
Empty set (0.01 sec)

mysql> select @@last_plan_from_binding;
+--------------------------+
| @@last_plan_from_binding |
+--------------------------+
|                        1 |
+--------------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)

mysql> select * from t where a in (1);
Empty set (0.00 sec)

mysql> select @@last_plan_from_binding;
+--------------------------+
| @@last_plan_from_binding |
+--------------------------+
|                        1 |
+--------------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [x] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
Fixed the bug for binding rule like in (1,2,3) cannot be applied to in (1)
```

symptoms: race = "on", | {"select 1 from b order by 2", "select ? from `b` order by 2"}, (parser/digester_test.go)
failure_signature: race = "on", | {"select 1 from b order by 2", "select ? from `b` order by 2"}, (parser/digester_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: race = "on", | {"select 1 from b order by 2", "select ?.
fix_pattern: race = "on", | {"select 1 from b order by 2", "select ? from `b` order by 2"}, (parser/digester_test.go)

evidence: evidence: race = "on", | {"select 1 from b order by 2", "select ? from `b` order by 2"}, (parser/digester_test.go)

files: bindinfo/capture_test.go, bindinfo/handle.go, bindinfo/handle_test.go, bindinfo/internal/testutil.go, bindinfo/tests/BUILD.bazel, bindinfo/tests/bind_test.go, ddl/ddl_worker.go, executor/adapter.go, executor/cluster_table_test.go, executor/internal/querywatch/query_watch.go, expression/builtin_info.go, parser/digester.go, parser/digester_test.go, parser/parser.y, planner/core/plan_cache_utils.go, planner/core/planbuilder.go, planner/core/preprocess.go, planner/optimize.go, server/conn.go, server/conn_stmt.go, session/bootstrap.go, session/nontransactional.go, session/session.go, session/txnmanager.go, sessionctx/stmtctx/stmtctx.go, tests/realtikvtest/pessimistictest/pessimistic_test.go, util/topsql/collector/mock/mock.go, util/util.go
